title: Bruins might have some tough decisions to make about Pavel Zacha this summer
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Pavel Zacha had a career year in 2025-26 with 30 goals and 65 points for the Boston Bruins.
Zacha's contract runs through 2026-27 at a $4.75 million cap hit, but a significant pay raise is expected.
The Bruins may consider trading Zacha for draft capital or talent, due to cap concerns and team retooling.

title: Berkshire County man charged with murder after body found in greenhouse
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Cole Bushnell, 41, of Sheffield was arraigned for murder after Michael Moore's body was found in his greenhouse.
Michael Moore, 40, of Winstead, Conn., was discovered dead with head trauma and a back puncture in Sheffield.
Bushnell and Moore, both painters, had a conflict over a shared job before the alleged murder occurred.

title: Pete Alonso homers for Orioles as Baltimore tops Red Sox 4-2
author: The Associated Press
bullets: Pete Alonso hit a two-run homer to lead the Baltimore Orioles to a 4-2 win over the Boston Red Sox.
Shane Baz pitched seven innings, allowing four hits, as the Orioles secured their third consecutive win.
Coby Mayo's homer was initially called a double before video review changed it, contributing to Baltimore's victory.

title: Mass. sheriffs’ offices run finances ‘like the wild west,’ Inspector General says
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts sheriffs' offices ended FY25 with a $110 million combined deficit due to budget mismanagement and lack of oversight.
Over 120 private bank accounts operated by sheriffs were uncovered, holding more than $36 million at the end of FY25.
The Inspector General's report highlights long-standing financial mismanagement and recommends reforms for consistent practices across sheriffs' offices.

title: Hampshire College warns final-year students may not be able to receive their degrees after all
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Hampshire College warns it may not be able to fulfill its plan for students to complete their degrees.
The college announced its closure after fall 2026, citing financial difficulties and insufficient funds for a teach-out.
Declining enrollment, failed asset sales, and unresolved debt have contributed to Hampshire College's financial challenges.

title: Mike Vrabel on Christian Gonzalez’s absence: ‘Contracts are part of professional sports’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Christian Gonzalez missed voluntary OTAs, raising speculation around his new contract with the New England Patriots.
Patriots coach Mike Vrabel emphasized contracts are business matters and hopes Gonzalez will attend next week's session.
Gonzalez's salary will increase from $2.2 million in 2026 to $18.1 million in 2027 under his fifth-year option.

title: What TreVeyon Henderson said about his viral Jaden Ivey tweet
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots' TreVeyon Henderson expressed support for Jaden Ivey after Ivey's anti-LGBTQ comments led the Bulls to waive him.
Henderson posted a Bible verse on X following criticism of Ivey, garnering nearly nine million views and mixed reactions.
Henderson discussed his post with coach Mike Vrabel, who supported his beliefs while emphasizing team inclusivity.

title: Here are the dates for this year’s Open Newbury Street
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Open Newbury Street will occur on 10 Sundays from July 12 to October 11, excluding some Sundays.
Newbury Street will be closed to vehicles from Massachusetts Avenue to Berkley Street from 10 a.m. to 6 p.m.
This year's schedule is adjusted due to the FIFA World Cup and bicentennial events, according to Mayor Wu.

title: 7 takeaways from A.J. Brown’s first press conference with the Patriots
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: A.J. Brown expressed excitement to join the Patriots, citing his childhood admiration for the team and Tom Brady.
Brown regretted not being drafted by the Patriots in 2019, when they chose N'Keal Harry over him.
Brown respects Julian Edelman and chose to wear No. 1 for the Patriots instead of taking Edelman's No. 11.

title: Police seek public’s help in finding missing 14-year-old Brighton boy
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police are searching for 14-year-old Fari Carless from Brighton, missing since May 1 near Jette Court.
Fari Carless is described as a 5’4” Black male with a thin build, long dreadlocks, and brown eyes.
Anyone with information on Carless's whereabouts is urged to contact Boston police detectives at 617-343-4256.
